Patch Set 3:

Huh, I thought this discussion was here...

This is sort of a repeat of Andreas' question:
Knowing that lockless I/O is broken right now (and that turning on lockless I/O is the normal goal/result of contention checking), what is the goal of this patch?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

yes, considering common problems with lock contention design these patches should not be considered for landing

commit.subject: LU-11435 ldlm: add contention check for IBITS locks
commit.message:
LU-11435 ldlm: add contention check for IBITS locks

Introduce contention check for IBITS locks, separate
processing of granted and waiting queues to it more
clear - waiting queue processing is simpler, it uses no
skiplists and needs no COS checks, etc.

Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: If477ac7994d9aa22ae9a54db1aba953c2165ccb8
